channel.title: Comments for page "Habil and Qabil"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Habil and Qabil"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 Immediatly

I can't believe this tale isn't more popular tbh, the way they apply the sitcom stereotypes to all the SCP while at the same time bringing up some of the most ridiculous characterizations these characters tend to attract is spot on, I really wish there was a part 2 where the Foundation discovers to their horror that a second season that was greenlit because the show was so bad that it was good.

My favorite part was the absolute randomness of the season finale:

Lord Blackwood: And that my friends is how Doctor Wondertainment learned the true value of Transcendental Meditation. Goodnight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would absolutely love to see an extremely low budget production of this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The best parts in my opinion
– hide block

The most notable scene is when SCP-1370 tries to attack Stella and Cybie, but due to its incompetence, they fail to realize it's trying to hurt them. They treat it like a doll and get it to accept the name of Princess Priscilla Pie.

The show then cuts to a live-action shot of Able who says "And that's what my life at Site 19 was like." The other characters stare at him blankly for a moment before Grabnok says "Bullshit."

Everything goes back to normal, except that Site 17 now has a Taco Tuesday.

At this point Dr. Locke starts ranting at SCP-049, pointing out how his costume is not historically accurate and how hammy his dialogue is. SCP-049 responds with "Good sir, my cure is most effective", much to the delight of the young researchers.
Dr. Locke punches SCP-049 in the face, and the episode ends.

Not gonna lie, but I'd definitely watch a show like this if someone made it. +1

Also, I feel like the part where the Junior Researcher's clamour to get 049 to say "I am the Cure" would play out in a similar vein to this scene from The Simpsons: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=37nwLhIA1zs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want it to be a real show +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Dr. Locke punches SCP-049 in the face, and the episode ends."

i feel like this after spending any amount of time on the SCP subreddit.

resounding +1, this entire article had me in stitches.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The junior researchers and SCP-049 episode is accurate

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really well done and good humor on this one.

I really wish people would put more of these 'Episode's' and see what happens to the alternatives of the characters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really want someone to make this an actual show/animation. Anyone want to do that? (I have neither the time nor the artistic ability.)
Oh, yeah, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cabal and Attaboy am filmerated afore a studio dance party, mashed potato! Cack!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thoroughly enjoyed this. Imagining the in-universe reactions of Foundation personnel only added to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of this a lot but honestly the in-universe referencing of skips as being from Series 1 right at the start is so jarring as to completely put me off upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even know what this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat, I remember when you posted this as a draft several months ago. I liked it then, and I still like it now.

Quibble
– hide block

I do miss the original draft's ending, with 073 repeatedly rewinding the last episode to hear his "brother"'s apology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what this is, but I liked it anyway. I probably shouldn't like it, but fuck it, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is a dumb reason for finally posting something that's been in my sandbox for over a year, but if Magnus can bring himself to post an SCP/MLP crossover then I at least have to give this a chance.

I will refrain from thanking my beta readers until I see how this is received.

Edit: Well it's a Boxing Day1 Miracle. +15 in 5 hours? Looks like this thing is staying up, and it's all Magnus's fault.

Mortos, Macleod, even though I consider this a shitpost I took your advice and removed the mention of Series I.

Now, I've had multiple people look at this over the past year and I didn't do a perfect job of keeping track of them, but thanks to Mr.Wrong, IHP, SunnyClockwork, Ekronak, Mendelssohn and apparently Nyka. If I left your name out I apologize.

Footnotes
1. For those not living in the British Commonwealth, Boxing Day is the day after Christmas, and that's about it
